posts.title: Pseudospectra Calculation
posts.text: Study, implementation, and parallelization of different algorithms to calculate the pseudospectrum of a matrix. Development of an interface allowing a user to manipulate the analysis of the pseudospectrum of a matrix. I will describe in more details later. Meanwhile you can check the project on this Github Link

pages.title: Welcome to my website
pages.text: Who am IMy name is Lasha Gogritchiani. In December 2025, I graduated with a Master of Computer Science, specifically the Cryptology, High-performance computing and Algorithms curriculum at Sorbonne University, which I completed on the “Excellency track.” Before that, I finished my Bachelor of Computer Science with a minor in Physics, also at Sorbonne University. I am particularly interested in post-quantum and quantum cryptography, implementation security (constant-time), and the efficiency of primitives. Currently, I am looking for a PhD or a full-time position at the intersection of at least two of these three interests. About this siteI decided to build this website mainly to discuss and gather my personal and professional projects. In my free time, I will also try to share and discuss aspects of cryptography and related topics in the form of short blog posts. The topics discussed may vary in difficulty; I will cover basics for quick reading to sensitize the general public to fundamental aspects of cryptography, as well as more advanced topics. Currently, the main sections of the website are organized as follows: CV: This section describes my educational and professional background. PROJECTS: A section dedicated to describe my projects. BLOG: Discussions on various topics, generally kept in a short-reading format. The rest of the site is mainly used for navigation. This website will be written primarily in English, but in some cases, I might also write blogs in French or Georgian. I will use dedicated tags for those languages.

pages.text: Professional ExperienceInternship | Product SecurityPQShield, France | 03/2025 – 09/2025 Designed and developed a tool in Python for the automatic detection of timing leaks in post-quantum algorithms. Implemented a C API enabling communication between the tool and cryptographic libraries. Skills: C, Python, Side-channel attacks, Post-quantum cryptography, Security Tooling. Skills: Interpersonal Skills, Teamwork, Communication, Organization.
